methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
NuScale Power operates in nuclear energy, a sector with limited comparable companies. The valuation universe comprises 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private competitors including Oklo, Nano Nuclear Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power. NuScale itself is public-stage, having raised over $600M to date.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. The nuclear energy sector contains only 8 same-sector comparables total, and filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation-tier peers yields fewer than three datapoints in each category—below the minimum threshold of three required for meaningful median calculation. Attempting to apply an all-sector median would introduce material bias given the heterogeneity of nuclear technology companies and their developmental timelines.

The limited comp set reflects structural constraints in the nuclear energy sector: few public benchmarks exist, private funding rounds are episodic and heavily contingent on regulatory and project-development milestones, and valuation drivers diverge significantly across small modular reactors, advanced reactors, and legacy nuclear companies. Without sufficient depth in the peer-median approach, alternative triangulation methods or direct project-based valuation frameworks would be required to support a valuation range. Key uncertainties include regulatory pathway clarity, capital intensity of commercialization, and the timing of revenue-generating project deployment across the peer set.
